preamble: The elision of the velar nasal [ŋ] is common in word-final or syllable-final positions, often following a stage of vowel nasalization or reduction.

examples.note: Common reduction where the velar nasal is fronted or elided after nasalizing the preceding vowel.
Complete loss of the nasal consonant following vowel nasalization.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
